mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail auto sales and decreases in the consumer price index indicate resilient consumer demand coupled with easing inflationary pressures, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which directly boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative correlation coefficient exceeding -0.10 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.3 million units annualized signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer leverage capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days due to anticipated economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.3 million units annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where housing starts breach the 1.3 million threshold, or the signal shows no predictive alpha after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, increasing consumer spending capacity and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4% month-over-month forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 65% of instances following CPI prints above the 0.4% threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 consistently.
